input.command:
kubectl get sts -n elasticsearch -o wide
echo "---pods---"
kubectl get pods -n elasticsearch -o wide
echo "---svc---"
kubectl get svc -n elasticsearch
echo "---cm---"
kubectl get cm -n elasticsearch
echo "---sts es-cluster full spec---"
kubectl get sts es-cluster -n elasticsearch -o yaml

  creationTimestamp: "2026-07-10T01:54:14Z"
  generation: 2
  labels:
    app: elasticsearch
  name: es-cluster
  namespace: elasticsearch
  resourceVersion: "18262"
  uid: a3b56222-10d5-4a7c-a48c-b29a4cdfc277
spec:
  persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy:
    whenDeleted: Retain
    whenScaled: Retain
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  serviceName: es-transport
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      containers:
      - env:
        - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: password
              name: elastic-password
        - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
          value: -Xms512m -Xmx512m
        image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        livenessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - bash
            - -c
            - curl -sk -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" --fail https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health
          failureThreshold: 6
          initialDelaySeconds: 60
          periodSeconds: 20
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 5
        name: elasticsearch
        ports:
        - containerPort: 9200
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 9300
          name: transport
          protocol: TCP
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - bash
            - -c
            - curl -sk -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" --fail https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health
          failureThreshold: 10
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 5
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "1"
            memory: 1536Mi
          requests:
            cpu: 250m
            memory: 1Gi
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
          name: data
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
          name: config
          subPath: elasticsearch.yml
        - mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
          name: certs
          readOnly: true
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext:
        fsGroup: 0
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
      volumes:
      - configMap:
          defaultMode: 420
          name: es-config
        name: config
      - name: certs
        secret:
          defaultMode: 288
          secretName: es-http-tls
  updateStrategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      partition: 0
    type: RollingUpdate
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - apiVersion: v1
    kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      name: data
    spec:
      accessModes:
      - ReadWriteOnce
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 3Gi
      storageClassName: standard
      volumeMode: Filesystem
    status:
      phase: Pending

input.command:
kubectl scale sts es-cluster -n elasticsearch --replicas=3
kubectl get sts es-cluster -n elasticsearch -w &
WPID=$!
sleep 15
kill $WPID 2>/dev/null
kubectl get pods -n elasticsearch -o wide

description:

kubectl get sts es-cluster -n elasticsearch -w &
WPID=$!
sleep 15
kill $WPID 2>/dev/null
kubectl get pods -n elasticsearch -o wide

content.text: New pods coming up. Let's wait for them to become Ready, and check if any external scaler is actively fighting us.

input.command:
kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready pod -l app=elasticsearch -n elasticsearch --timeout=180s 2>&1
kubectl get sts es-cluster -n elasticsearch
kubectl get pods -n elasticsearch -o wide

content.text: All 3 original nodes are Ready. Let's verify cluster health and check current node attributes before designing the new node group.

input.command:
ELASTIC_PW=$(kubectl get secret elastic-password -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d)
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch curl-test -- curl -sk -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PW}" "https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local:9200/_cluster/health?pretty" 2>&1
echo "=== nodes attrs ==="
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch curl-test -- curl -sk -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PW}" "https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local:9200/_cat/nodeattrs?v" 2>&1
echo "=== indices ==="
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch curl-test -- curl -sk -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PW}" "https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local:9200/_cat/indices?v" 2>&1
